heading: Research and extension projects
label: 5811 Research and extension projects

children.label: A
children.text: reduce, to the extent feasible and practicable, the use of chemical pesticides, fertilizers, and toxic natural materials in agricultural production;

children.label: B
children.text: improve low-input farm management to enhance agricultural productivity, profitability, and competitiveness; and

children.label: C
children.text: promote crop, livestock, and enterprise diversification; and

children.label: A
children.text: study, to the extent practicable, agricultural production systems that are located in areas that possess various soil, climate, and physical characteristics;

children.label: B
children.text: study farms that have been, and will continue to be, managed using farm production practices that rely on low-input and conservation practices;

children.label: C
children.text: take advantage of the experience and expertise of farmers and ranchers through their direct participation and leadership in projects;

children.label: D
children.text: transfer practical, reliable and timely information to farmers and ranchers concerning low-input sustainable farming practices and systems; and

children.label: E
children.text: promote a partnership between farmers, nonprofit organizations, agribusiness, and public and private research and extension institutions.

children.heading: Agreements
children.label: b Agreements
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out this section through agreements entered into with land-grant colleges or universities, other universities, State agricultural experiment stations, the State cooperative extension services, nonprofit organizations with demonstrable expertise, or Federal or State governmental entities.

children.label: A
children.text: the relevance of the project to the purposes of this part;

children.label: B
children.text: the appropriateness of the design of the project;

children.label: C
children.text: the likelihood of obtaining the objectives of the project; and

children.label: D
children.text: the national or regional applicability of the findings and outcomes of the proposed project.

children.label: A
children.text: closely coordinate research and extension activities;

children.label: B
children.text: indicate the manner in which the findings of the project will be made readily usable by farmers;

children.label: C
children.text: maximize the involvement and cooperation of farmers, including projects involving on-farm research and demonstration;

children.label: D
children.text: involve a multidisciplinary systems approach; and

children.label: E
children.text: involve cooperation between farms, non-profit organizations, colleges and universities, and government agencies.

children.heading: Diversification of research
children.label: d Diversification of research
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct projects and studies under this section in areas that are broadly representative of the diversity of United States agricultural production, including production on family farms, mixed-crop livestock farms and dairy operations.

children.heading: On-farm research
children.label: e On-farm research
children.text: The Secretary may conduct projects and activities that involve on-farm research and demonstration in carrying out this section.

children.heading: Impact studies
children.label: f Impact studies
children.text: The Secretary may approve study projects concerning the national and regional economic, global competitiveness, social and environmental implications of the adoption of low-input sustainable agricultural practices and systems.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may approve projects to be conducted under this section that have a duration of more than one fiscal year.

children.heading: Sequence planting
children.label: 2 Sequence planting
children.text: In the case of a research project conducted under this section that involves the planting of a sequence of crops or crop rotations, the Secretary shall approve such projects for a term that is appropriate to the sequence or rotation being studied.

children.heading: Public access
children.label: h Public access
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that research projects conducted under this section are open for public observation at specified times.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), the Secretary may indemnify the operator of a project conducted under this section for damage incurred or undue losses sustained as a result of a rigid requirement of research or demonstration under such project that is not experienced in normal farming operations.

children.heading: Subject to agreement
children.label: 2 Subject to agreement
children.text: An indemnity payment under paragraph (1) shall be subject to any agreement between a project grantee and operator entered into prior to the initiation of such project.
